title: Documentation
description: Write a concise `README.md` covering: installation (`pip install -e .`), the core editing workflow (unpack → edit XML → clean → pack), a usage example for each command showing both JSON and --plain output, and a note on the JSON-first output contract. No API docs needed — CLI --help is the API reference.
acceptance_criteria: `README.md` covers installation, the unpack→edit→clean→pack workflow, and all eight command groups.
Each command section shows at least one example invocation with its expected JSON stdout.
The --plain flag is explained with an example.
The output contract (JSON default, --plain opt-out, errors to stderr, exit codes) is documented.
README renders without errors in standard Markdown.

handoff_summary.completed: README.md created with all required sections: installation (pip and uv), core editing workflow, output contract (JSON default, --plain opt-out, stderr errors, exit codes), and full per-command documentation for extract-text, unpack, pack, clean, slide list, slide layouts, slide add, slide delete, and slide move — each with at least one JSON example and a --plain example.
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. Documentation is aligned with validated runtime behavior read directly from the source files.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — README.md is a new file with no overlap with any other in-flight bead.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Documented extract-text without a JSON wrapper when --output is omitted (raw text to stdout), matching actual CLI behavior. Counted 9 commands across 4 top-level commands and 5 slide subcommands to cover "all eight command groups" exhaustively.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: No tests written or run; documentation bead only.

conflict_risks: None — README.md is a new file with no overlap with any other in-flight bead.

execution_history.summary: Wrote README.md covering installation, the unpack→edit→clean→pack workflow, output contract, and all nine commands with JSON and --plain examples.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
